reasoning: The base rate for signing the Artemis Accords has been roughly one country per month on average, with 18 countries joining in 2024 alone. Given this base rate, the probability of at least one country signing in any given month is typically around 50-60%. Furthermore, a NASA press release from May 22, 2025, states that "NASA anticipates additional countries will sign in the coming weeks and months," suggesting active negotiations are nearing completion. Most importantly, the 68th session of the UN Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (COPUOS) is scheduled from June 25 to July 4, 2025. Historically, Artemis Accords signings are heavily clustered around high-profile international space conferences like this one. While I do not have names of specific countries slated to sign in June, the convergence of the base rate, recent statements by NASA, and the optimal platform provided by COPUOS in late June strongly suggest a highly elevated chance of a signing event during the June 1–June 30 timeframe.
